design_signals: Compact, dense UI chrome — maximum space for the canvas
Left panel for layers, right panel for properties — classic CAD spatial model
Dark sidebar with neutral content area — reduces distraction from the work
Keyboard shortcut system visible everywhere — tool respects power users
Real-time collaboration indicators (avatars) in the toolbar — presence as a feature
design_lesson: When the product IS the canvas, the UI must disappear. Every pixel of chrome is a pixel of distraction.
type_direction: Inter (system-forward). Compact, dense. Tabular numbers for coordinates and measurements.
color_direction: Near-black sidebar (#1e1e1e), white canvas, accent only for selection and interactive states. Status colors strictly for collaboration avatars and component states.

design_signals: Keyboard-first — every action has a shortcut, Cmd+K opens a full command palette
Near-pixel-perfect alignment — every element on exactly the right grid position
Minimal color usage — status represented by icons and muted semantic colors, not bright fills
Frictionless issue creation — one keyboard shortcut, minimal required fields
Dark mode as default, light mode as option — developer audience expectation
design_lesson: Removing every unnecessary click is a product feature, not just a UX preference. The best interaction is no interaction.
type_direction: Inter, compact weights, mono for IDs and codes.
color_direction: Near-black dark mode (#0f0f0f), neutral gray palette, single vivid accent. Status colors desaturated — important enough to notice, calm enough not to alarm.

design_signals: Block-based document metaphor — the entire product is an extension of a blank page
Progressive disclosure everywhere — features hide until needed, surface when relevant
Neutral chrome that matches document aesthetics — UI and content share the same visual language
Slash command interface — actions discovered in context, not in menus
Cover images and icons personalise without forcing structure
design_lesson: Hiding complexity is not the same as removing it. Notion teaches that progressive disclosure enables both beginners and experts in the same interface.
type_direction: Default sans (Georgia serif for doc mode). Generous line-height. Large, readable body text.
color_direction: White/off-white default. Neutral sidebar (#f7f7f5). Accent only for links and interactive elements. Color used for page icons and covers, not for chrome.

design_signals: Warm, rounded aesthetic — oval shapes, no sharp corners anywhere
Muted earthy palette — orange, warm beige, deep navy — calming without being clinical
Generous whitespace — slow, deliberate pace communicated through space
Playful illustrations without being childish — friendly, approachable expertise
Single-focus screens — one content item or action at a time
design_lesson: Emotional design for sensitive domains (health, mental wellness) requires every element to reinforce safety. Rounded shapes, warm colors, and breathing room are not decorative — they're clinical.
type_direction: Rounded sans-serif (similar to GT Walsheim). Warm, soft weight. Large, unhurried body text.
color_direction: Warm orange accent (#FF7B54-range). Deep navy and warm white backgrounds. No stark black/white. Semantic colors softened (no angry red).

design_signals: Black and white as a full design language — not a limitation but a statement
Monochrome palette forces hierarchy through spacing and size, not color
Data-forward dashboard — deployment status, performance metrics, edge function logs front and center
Code always visible — terminal output, build logs, function traces are hero content
Micro-animations for deploy status — loading states as performance art
design_lesson: Constraint breeds creativity. When you remove color as a tool, spacing and typography must carry all hierarchy — the result is a product that looks inevitable, not designed.
type_direction: Geist (proprietary, similar to Inter). Mono for all code/logs. Heavy type weights for KPIs.
color_direction: Black (#000), white (#fff), gray scale. One accent color only — electric blue or green for active/live states. Semantic colors standard (red/green/yellow for deploy status).

design_signals: Polaris design system — highly consistent, component-driven, merchant-focused
Business-first dashboard — revenue, orders, products always center stage
Progressive complexity — simple store setup, advanced inventory/shipping hidden until needed
Merchant onboarding: guided checklist visible until complete — celebrates each step
Trust-first tone in copy — never assumes merchant expertise, explains consequences clearly
emotional_effect: Trusted partner, business confidence, supportive system
design_lesson: B2B products that serve non-technical users (small business owners) must balance power with guidance. Shopify shows you don't have to choose — progressive disclosure enables both.
type_direction: Inter or system font. Readable at all sizes. Clear heading hierarchy for settings and navigation.
color_direction: Green (#008060 brand green) for primary actions. Neutral dashboard palette. Semantic semantic colors. No color for decoration — every color choice is functional.

design_signals: Maximum information density — every pixel occupied with data
Monochrome + semantic color only — no decorative color ever
Keyboard-first interaction — terminal commands, function keys, no mouse required
Real-time data update everywhere — the UI is always moving
Color semantics: orange = Bloomberg brand, red = negative, green = positive, white on black = base
design_lesson: For power users in time-critical environments, efficiency IS the UX. Every concession to aesthetics or discoverability is a tradeoff against speed. Know your user and serve them without compromise.
type_direction: Monospace throughout. Small sizes (12–14px standard). Function key labels. No web fonts.
color_direction: Black background, white text, orange brand, green positive, red negative. Zero decorative color. Maximum semantic precision.

design_signals: Extreme whitespace — product photography breathes, no chrome competes
Minimal navigation — luxury signals restraint, not abundance
Typography as identity — editorial serif headlines, whisper-quiet captions
Product is the content — the entire UI is a frame for photography
No promotional banners, countdown timers, or urgency signals — luxury rejects pressure
design_lesson: Luxury products communicate value through restraint. Less chrome, more space, fewer words. The absence of noise is the message.
type_direction: Serif display typeface (Canela-range). Light weight. Generous tracking. No bold weights for body text.
color_direction: White dominant. Black text. No accent color — navigation and interactive elements are black/dark gray. Zero semantic color for decorative purposes.
